behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all four affected files and the specific methods (CheckPermission, CheckMediaAccessPermission, CheckSerialAccessPermission, CanRequestPortPermission) that tests inspect.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug (main frame origin used instead of requesting frame) without specifying exact code changes or signatures.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact literals like 'CheckSerialAccessPermission(frame)', 'requesting_frame->GetLastCommittedOrigin().GetURL().spec()', and exact parameter name 'requesting_frame' as first parameter, forbidding alternative valid names/approaches.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All f2p tests are regex/substring checks on source files (e.g., 're.search(pattern, content)'); no compilation or runtime behavior is tested — only clang-format/cpplint subprocess runs.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific signature changes and origin derivation from requesting_frame that a stub cannot satisfy without implementing the fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction's described behavior (frame origin propagation across the three methods and their callers) is covered by the test assertions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are individually named functions with docstrings labeling F2P/P2P categorization.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a C++ code fix, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and method names match between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction identifies affected files and methods at an appropriate level since localization is part of the task context, without pointing to line numbers.
